figures.caption: Figure 2: A two-dimensional model of the non-uniform slope being an inclined plane as in Figure 1, with a space-dependent traction coefficient η(x) ∈ [0, 1] in addition (marked illustratively by the color-coded slope). The varying traction will influence the behaviour of time-minimizing geodesics and related time fronts; see the specific examples on further reading (Sections 4.2 and 4.3).

Figure 3: Left: The stream density plot of the ordinary wind W (x, y) given by (55), i.e., the superwind in the Zermelo case that acts on the inclined plane S. The color-coded background represents the wind “force”, satisfying the strong convexity condition for all (x, y) ∈ S, i.e., ||W ||h ⩽ √ 6 5 ≈ 0.4899 < 1 2 . Right: The contour plot of Wη=0 = W , as on the left.

Figure 8: Top left: The time front centered at (0, 0) under the action of the gravitational wind Gη considered as a particular superwind and the space-dependent traction coefficient (56) (red; note its lack of x-symmetry), compared to the edge cases, i.e., η = 0 (blue, ZNP) and η = 1 (green, MAT); t = 3. Bottom left: As above (red) and compared with the cases η = 3 4 (black) and η = 1 2 (dashed), together with the respective geodesics drawn with the step of ∆θ = π 8 , i.e., 16 time-minimizing paths. Note that the time-minimizing geodesics for the varying η (red) are not the Euclidean straight lines, although they look to be so due to the relatively slight changes. In contrast, the Finslerian geodesics for both η = 3 4 (black) and η = 1 2 (dashed lines) are the Euclidean straight lines; t = 3. Bottom right: The change of the ratio x′(t)

Figure 7: Left: The deforming factors on the inclined plane S: the gravitational wind Gη considered as a particular superwind, where W = Gη=0 = GT blows in the steepest downhill direction (black arrows) of the slope and the space-dependent traction coefficient is given by (56) (the colorcoded background); ḡ = 1.115 < √ 5 2 ≈ 1.118. Right: A comparison of the Finslerian indicatrices (limaçons) in the tangent plane y1Oy2, centered at O = (0, 0), under Gη and varying η(x, y) (red), η = 1 (MAT, green), η = 0 (ZNP, blue), η = 3 4 (black) and the Riemannian case (yellow); t = 1. Note the y1-axial symmetry of the indicatrix referring to the varying traction (red) which coincides herein with the indicatrix in the presence of constant traction on the entire slope, i.e., η = 1 2 , since η(0, 0) = 1 2 . The craft’s own velocity is denoted by u (dashed brown), with ||u||h = 1.

Figure 14: The comparisons of the time-minimizing geodesics (t = 7) and related time fronts (t ∈ {3 (innermost), 5 (middle), 7 (outermost)}) under the influence of the superwind Wη, where Wη=0 = W is given by (55) and various tractions, i.e., η(x, y) as in (57) (red), η ∈ {0 (blue, ZNP), 1 2 (cyan), 3 4 (black), 1 (green, MAT)}, where the initial point is located at (0, 0)

Figure 12: The time fronts for: the varying traction coefficient (57) (red), η = 0 (blue, ZNP), η = 1 (green, MAT) and the unperturbed Riemannian case (yellow); t = 2. The time-minimizing geodesics for η(x, y) are drawn in red with a step of ∆θ = π 8 . In the background, the color-coded wind “force” ||W ||h (left), where W is given by (55) (black arrows), and density plot of the spacedependent traction coefficient (57) (right).
Figure 13: The analogous scenario presented respectively as in Figure 11 (left) and Figure 12 (right), where the initial point is located at ( 3 4 , 3 4 ), for comparison.

Figure 5: Left: The time-minimizing geodesics and time front centered at (0, 0) under the action of the superwind Wη and constant traction η = 3 4 (black) on the inclined plane S, compared with the edge cases: η = 0 (blue, ZNP) and η = 1 (green, MAT) as well as the Riemannian isochrone (yellow), where the related ordianry wind (in the Zermelo sense) reads (55) (its action is indicated by black arrows); t = 5. Right: The behaviour of the time-minimizing geodesics (η = 3 4 ) and evolution of the corresponding time fronts for t ∈ {3 (innermost), 5 (middle), 7 (outermost)} (color-coded as on the left). The geodesics are drawn with a step of ∆θ = π 8 , i.e., 16 timeminimizing paths in both subfigures.

Figure 6: Left: The time-minimizing geodesics and time fronts centered at (0, 0) under the action of the superwind related to (55) (grey arrows), for different types of η-navigation on S, i.e., η ∈ {0 (ZNP, blue), 3 4 (black), 1 (MAT, green)}; t = 3. The geodesics are presented with a step of ∆θ = π 8 and the unperturbed Riemannian isochrone is marked in yellow. Right: As on the left, with the color-coded background that represents the space-dependent wind norm ||W ||h; t = 3.

Figure 1: Left: A two-dimensional model being an inclined plane M of the slope angle ε in R3, under the action of the anisotropic superwind Wη, where the space-dependent ordinary wind W (i.e., in the Zermelo sense; blue) has an arbitraty direction and norm (weak); O ∈ M . The resultant velocity is represented by vη, including the boundary cases for η ∈ {0, 1}, i.e., the Zermelo case (vZNP , light blue) and the Matsumoto case (vMAT , green), respectively. The transverse (cross) component −−→

Figure 11: Left: The time-minimizing geodesics and time fronts for the varying traction (57) (red) on S, together with the time fronts for η = 0 (blue, ZNP), η = 1 (green, MAT) and the unperturbed Riemannian case (yellow); t = 2. The initial points is positioned at (0, 0). In the background, the action of the vector field (55) (black arrows) and space-dependent traction coefficient η(x, y) (pastel colors). Right: The same scenario as on the left, presented on the planar map and with the respective geodesics in addition, drawn with a step of ∆θ = π 8 ; t = 5.

Figure 10: Left: A stream plot of the ordinary wind in the Zermelo sense W (x, y) = Wη=0 (black arrows) as in (55) and density plot of the space-dependent traction coefficient (57) (the color-coded background) on S. Right: A contour plot of the varying traction coefficient η(x, y) ∈ (0, 1] given by (57), as on the left.

Figure 4: Left: The Finslerian indicatrices centered at O = (0, 0) in the tangent plane y1Oy2 under the action of the superwind Wη (solid), where the space-dependent wind Wη=0 = W (x, y) is given by (55), in the presence of constant tractions, i.e., η ∈ {0 (blue, ZNP), 3 4 (black), 1 (green, MAT)}. For comparison, the initial Riemannian indicatrix being the elliptical h-circle (yellow) in the absence of superwind and the common Euclidean circle (dashed grey). Right: As on the left and compared in addition with the Finslerian indicatrices deformed under a different superwind being the gravitational wind Gη (dashed), where ḡ = 1.115 < √ 5 2 ≈ 1.118, satisfying the condition for strong convexity ||GT ||h < 1 2 . In contrast to W , Gη=0 = GT blows in a fixed direction (steepest descent) and has a constant norm on S, i.e., ||GT ||h = 0.49.

Figure 9: A comparison of time fronts for t = 3: with the space-dependent traction coefficient (56) (red), Riemannian (yellow), η = 0 (ZNP, blue), η = 1 2 (dashed cyan), η = 3 4 (black), η = 1 (MAT, green), under the influence of the gravitational wind Gη (black arrows) acting in the steepest downhill direction, which is considered as a particular superwind in the current study; ḡ = 1.115 < √ 5 2 ≈ 1.118. The isochrone corresponding the varying η (red) is not symmetric w.r.t. the axis x, unlike all other time fronts for η = const.
